title: Dear Annie: How do I get my in-laws to stop hurling insults my way without blowing up the family?
COLUMNIST: Where’s the beef?
description: When I was in grade school back in the 1800s, they fed us cheeseburgers extended to the frontiers of human palatability by hefty amounts of what some call "textured vegetable protein;" all the kids knew was soy. Nobody liked them. But Americans might start considering soy burgers this summer when they see what happens to the price of beef.
